title: Senior Manager - Talent Development
description:
Key Responsibilities:

Talent Development & Learning Strategy

  • Lead the design and execution of company-wide skill development and upskilling frameworks.
  • Drive and conduct leadership and management training programs aligned with business strategy and future capabilities.
  • Oversee career planning and personal development initiatives to support individual growth and internal mobility.
  • Manage the Young Talent Development and Management Trainee Programme to build a strong leadership pipeline.
  • Oversee and refine onboarding and orientation programs to ensure seamless integration of new hires.
  • Conduct strategic learning needs analysis and develop individual development plans for succession readiness.

Employee Experience & Engagement

  • Champion a positive and inclusive workplace culture that reflects company values and supports employee well-being.
  • Design and implement employee engagement strategies, including 360-degree feedback, surveys, and action planning.
  • Partner with business leaders to identify and address organizational development needs.
  • Use data-driven insights to continuously improve the employee lifecycle experience.
  • Any other duties as assigned.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree in human resources, Psychology, Organizational Development, Business, or related field
  • Minimum 8 years of progressive HR experience, with a strong focus on learning and development and employee engagement.
  • Proven leadership experience in managing cross-functional programs and teams.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Expertise in talent frameworks, leadership development, and organizational cultural transformation.
  • Strong proficiency in English and Chinese is required, as the role involves regular communication with stakeholders, regional teams, and headquarters where both languages are used. The ability to communicate effectively across cultures is important for delivering training, facilitating discussions, and aligning talent initiatives across global operations.

postedCompany.description:
China United Lines (CULINES) was established in 2005 with its headquarters in Singapore. We have been focusing on both short and long-haul trades. In 2021, we expanded into TP and European markets. Over the years we have established an international network covering coastal ports in Chinese mainland and other countries such as Hong Kong, Taiwan, Japan, South Korea, Southeast Asia, South Asia, and the Middle East. We also have a comprehensive agency network comprising of 3rd party, joint ventures and fully owned agents that support providing customers with high quality and value-added logistics services.


We are equipped with an international management team, excellent IT system, and digital service platform. As a socially responsible enterprise, we will continue to provide customers and partners with efficient, economical, and reliable services and create more values to benefit the society. During 2021, our Alphaliner ranking has improved from No. 79 to No. 23 and we became the second Chinese mainland-based carrier offering regular China-U.S. and China-Europe services. 


With operations spanning major trade routes across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and North America, CU LINES offers employees a truly global perspective. You’ll work with international teams, gain exposure to diverse markets, and play a critical role in connecting global trade.


In 2022, let’s go hand-in-hand towards a new chapter of CULINES! All talents around the world are welcome to join our team! If you are the one we are looking for, please do not hesitate to contact us.
postedCompany.name: CU LINES PTE. LTD.
